questions.question: Which cryptography type uses the same key for encryption and decryption?
questions.description: Symmetric encryption uses one shared secret key for encryption and decryption.

questions.question: Which function is designed to be one-way?
questions.description: Hash functions produce fixed-length digests and should be difficult to reverse.

questions.question: Which algorithm is an asymmetric cryptography algorithm?
questions.description: RSA uses a public/private key pair.
